author: Steadying
text:
Drop the force-rewrite prohibition from §16 migration cutover

The cutover line erected exactly the safety-control the project subtracts
elsewhere (cwd-guard, install confirmation flag): 'the published
origin/balls/tasks MUST NOT be force-rewritten ... a coordinated push, not
a force-rewrite.' It contradicted §6's own stance for install ('the commit
IS the undo; git is the recovery net').

Replaced with the honest framing: a federated cutover is just bl install
(its per-op store sync, §12 'syncs every op, default ON', pushes it — no
separate push step), and the push may force-rewrite the shared ref. That is
intrinsic to a one-time format migration, recoverable via git, with an
optional git branch balls-archive first. The runbook stays bl-0802.

bl-6965 (Center spec) resolves with NO new section: 'center' is already
specified as emergent in §12 ('No branch declares itself a center'); a
dedicated § would re-home what §12 owns (SSOT, §0). The only live residue
was this subtraction.
